nodes.name: WhatsApp Webhook
nodes.notes: Meta sends all incoming WhatsApp messages here. Configure this URL in your Meta App Dashboard under Webhooks.

nodes.name: Text Messages Only
nodes.notes: Filter out non-text messages (images, voice notes, etc.) — handle those separately if needed.

nodes.name: Extract Message Fields
nodes.notes: Pulls out the sender number, message text, and metadata from the WhatsApp payload.

nodes.name: Load Conversation History
nodes.notes: Loads the last N messages for this WhatsApp number from Redis. This gives Maria conversation memory. If you don't have Redis, replace with a Firestore read node.

nodes.name: Build Message History
nodes.notes: Prepares the messages array — conversation history + new message — for the AI API.

nodes.name: Ask Claude (Maria)
nodes.notes: Calls the Claude API. Uses claude-haiku-4-5 for speed + cost. Swap to claude-sonnet-4-6 for higher quality responses if budget allows. Replace this node with the OpenAI or Gemini node if preferred.

nodes.name: Send WhatsApp Reply
nodes.notes: Sends Maria's reply back via the Meta WhatsApp Cloud API.

nodes.name: Save Conversation History
nodes.notes: Saves the updated conversation history to Redis with a 7-day TTL. Replace with Firestore if you're using Firebase.

nodes.name: Prepare Firebase Log
nodes.notes: Prepares the lead data for Firebase. Connect this to an HTTP Request node pointing at your Firebase REST endpoint.

nodes.name: Log to Firebase
nodes.notes: Replace YOUR_PROJECT_ID with your actual Firebase project ID. This creates a new document in the whatsapp_leads collection.

nodes.name: Respond to Meta
nodes.notes: Meta requires a 200 OK response within 15 seconds. This sends it immediately regardless of processing status.
